mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism generates negative forward returns in >55% of post-shock recovery periods, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns turns negative during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and corporate hiring confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of periods with positive PAYEMS growth, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.0 year-over-year, persistent inflation forces monetary tightening expectations and erodes real corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation periods, or the threshold fails to predict return direction with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of tightening-cycle periods with declining INDPRO, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.
